title: Smart boiler control interface
text:
Ansys SCADE Ansys SCADE Suite, Display, Test version MIT License
The Ansys SCADE Suite® and Ansys SCADE Display® software were used to automatically generate this
example of a smart boiler control interface. This modern interface incorporates touch interactivity
concepts into a smart boiler control panel.
With this interface, you can control the water level in a steam boiler. It is important that this
interface works correctly to control the quantity of water present in the steam boiler when the
steam boiler is operating. If the water quantity is too low or too high, the smart boiler could
be seriously affected.
This example serves as a companion to SCADE Smart Boiler Control - Designing a next-gen embedded HMI,
which is a part of Ansys’ embedded Human Machine Interface (HMD) design blog series.
Smart boiler control interface

text:
The following image provides a brief of how collaborative efforts and the power

Brief

title: Contribute
text:
Overall guidance on contributing to a PyAnsys library appears in
Contributing
in the PyAnsys developer’s guide. Ensure that you are thoroughly familiar
with this guide before attempting to contribute to the Ansys SCADE smart boiler
control example.
The following contribution information is specific to the Ansys SCADE smart boiler
control interface.
Contribute

title: Contribute > Install in developer mode
text:
Installing the Ansys SCADE smart boiler control interface in developer mode allows you to modify the
source and enhance it.
Clone the ansys-scade-example-smart-boiler-control repository:
Access the scade-example-smart-boiler-control directory where the repository has been cloned:
Create a clean Python 3.10 environment and activate it:
You should use the interpreter delivered with Ansys SCADE. For example,
C:\Program Files\ANSYS Inc\v232\SCADE\contrib\Python310\python.exe.
Make sure that you have the latest required build system, documentation, testing,
and CI tools:
Install in developer mode
ansys-scade-example-smart-boiler-control
scade-example-smart-boiler-control
C:\Program Files\ANSYS Inc\v232\SCADE\contrib\Python310\python.exe

title: Contribute > Use pre-commit
text:
The example for the Ansys SCADE boiler plate interface follows the PEP8 standard as outlined in
PEP 8 in
the PyAnsys developer’s guide and implements style checking using
pre-commit.
To ensure your code meets minimum code styling standards, run these commands:
You can also install this as a pre-commit hook by running this command:
This way, it’s not possible for you to push code that fails the style checks:
Use pre-commit
pre-commit

title: Contribute > Build documentation
text:
To build documentation, you can run these commands:
Build documentation

title: Contribute > Post issues
text:
Use the Issues
page for this repository to report bugs and request new features. When possible,
use the issue templates provided. If your issue does not fit into one of these templates,
click the link for opening a blank issue.
If you have general questions about the PyAnsys ecosystem, email
pyansys.core@ansys.com. If your
question is specific to the Ansys SCADE smart boiler
control interface, ask your question in an issue as described
in the previous paragraph.
Post issues

title: Getting started > Install
text:
To install the smart boiler control interface, run this command to clone the
repository
to your local machine
Install

title: Getting started > Generate, build, and execute standalone interface
section: Generate, build, and execute standalone interface
text:
Launch SCADE Display.
Select File > Open and then the model/scade-display/DisplayPanel.etp
project.
Select the Windows configuration to enable the generation of a standalone application
running on Windows.
Click Execute.
Generate, build, and execute standalone interface
model/scade-display/DisplayPanel.etp
Windows

title: Getting started > Test execution on host
text:
Launch SCADE Test.
Select File > Open and then the model/scade-test/SmartBoilerControl_Test.etp
SCADE test projet.
Select Project > Test tool > Execute tests.
Test execution on host
model/scade-test/SmartBoilerControl_Test.etp
